mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC > 0.05 or positive 20d returns occur in >55% of high-rate observations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing consumer spending expectations and triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon through improved corporate revenue forecasts.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60d returns in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above its 12-month moving average during a late-cycle expansion phase reflects peak manufacturing capacity utilization, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum yields negative 20d returns in >55% of late-cycle observations.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding 0.3% triggers expectations of central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of high-inflation trigger events.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, boosting earnings growth expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Divergent PAYEMS and UNRATE trends or joint positive labor signals fail to predict positive 20d returns with Rank IC > 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 2.0% establishes a baseline of expanding economic output, directly supporting corporate profit growth and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth > 2.0% correlates with negative 60d returns in >50% of quarterly observations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production decline exceeding 0.5% signals abrupt supply-side contraction, reducing near-term corporate output expectations and triggering negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered contraction events.
